_comment: Inspect descriptions. Keys are 'tile:<TileVariant>' for overworld tiles and 'furn:<Furn>' for house furniture. Missing keys fall back to '(no description)'.
tile:DoorRod: The Rod Shop door. Press f to enter.
tile:DoorSchool: The Fishing School door. Press f to enter.
tile:DoorHouse: A house door. Press f to enter.
tile:MineEntrance: A mineshaft entrance. Press f to descend.
tile:MineFrame: Timber bracing at the shaft mouth.
tile:MineExit: Wooden rungs upward. Press f to climb out.
tile:InfernoWall: A basalt wall, warm to the touch.
tile:InfernoFloor: Cracked ground; faint glow between the seams.
tile:DimPortal: A dimensional passage. Step on, press f to use.
tile:Smelter: A smelter. Press f to smelt ore into ingots.
tile:Forge: A forge. Press f to forge equipment.
furn:Exit: Front door. Step on it to leave.
faceless:blessing: {name}: "Hey there, friend of Borin. Many blessings on your spelunking."
faceless:curse: Faceless: "YOU ARE ONE OF BORIN THE CANNIBAL. DEVOURED THAT WHICH IS DEAREST TO ME. SUFFER."
faceless:inspect: A faceless figure shuffling in the dark.
_curios_comment: Inspectable curios scattered sparsely across every dimension (~1 per 700 tiles). Engine places them; user writes the prose. Each cell hosts the same curio every time you visit (hashed on x/y/dim/seed). Replace the TODO strings below with whatever lore/flavor you want; the player reads this when standing in front of one and pressing x.
curio:driftwood: almost soft piece of wood. everyone that glances at it immediately looks away. they all know whose boat it was. with so little of us, we count every loss.
curio:seaglass: the high salt concentration isn't enough to explain the texture of it. the truth is, the sea has an almost abrasive quality.
curio:cairn: they say stacked stones are bad, breaks the "leave no trace principle"...
curio:broken-buoy: the sulfur from the seas seems to have reacted with it, it smells horrible, it's turned a yellow-ish green.
curio:memorial-stone: one of many. barely thousands, soon hundreds.
curio:crow-feather: terribly rare. no one even knows where they came from, we see no birds. it is said that when everyone's eyes are closed, crows fly over the village.
curio:salt-crust: salt is highly valuable. the water has too many dangerous compounds dissolved, mainly sulfur.
curio:rust-helmet: the rust is flaking off. lost helmets are a mixed signal, you don't know if it's a lost human, or a heathen eliminated. you don't know what to feel.
curio:coal-heap: asked a faceless to help them carry it.
curio:skeleton-bits: the flesh came off easy as b's teeth tore through it. you remember his smile in the village. you feel terrible yet you know you have to be nice to him.
curio:tally-marks: these are not counting days. bon appetit, b.
curio:mineshaft-sign: keep your eyes low. do not talk.
curio:sunken-statue: they say the coelecanth are granted immortality by poseidon. this one's was revoked.
curio:ancient-amphora: sealed jar, mostly air inside. the few drops inside are really drops of the first water to ever grace gaia.
curio:pearl-bed: merchants use these pearls for trade. you better not touch them.
curio:whale-bone: gigantic bones of an old whale khei priest.
curio:coral-skull: skull encrusted with barnacles. he learned the truth of the elders.
curio:sea-shrine: you think about dropping a coin. you know your currency would give away your nature. you would be killed.
curio:ship-anchor: this anchor literally holds the city in place. cut it and Atlantis drifts free.
curio:obsidian-shard: tear of the Fallen Fish, a shard of obsidian.
curio:bone-pile: they listened to dante, yet they couldn't keep their rod steady against the well's pull.
curio:brimstone-vent: the place where water touched lava, yet no obsidian formed. a vent of brimstone.
curio:lava-glass: lava that cooled at the right pace to turn into a transparent type of obsidian. those who can't see value these wildly.
curio:rusted-chain: the chain of anchors found in atlantis. binds cities.
curio:broken-crate: someone tried to smuggle some goods. was caught and swallowed. this must have been a dropoff point.
curio:rat-skull: crazy? i was crazy once. they put me in a room. a skull of a rat here? what? how?
curio:pipe-fragment: broken off the well system. could have something to do with the well's weirdness.
curio:coin-pile: a threat, or some money left at a dropoff point for some shady figure to find.
curio:graffiti-slab: words scratched into stone. they say this is where those without faces are initiated.
curio:soggy-paper: cryptic writing, but you can discern some heart symbols. a romance which had to be hidden. their message lost. you imagine what both the sender and receiver felt.
curio:bath-stone: absorbs sulfur from the water. its surface darkens with what it pulls out.
curio:mineral-cake: solid salt-vein. high value if you can pry it loose.
curio:bamboo-basin: still fills, still drips. the bather uses it daily.
curio:offering-bowl: the springs heal you. they ask shed skin in return.
curio:steam-shrine: the gas is... liquid..? you see drops floating among the heaps of steam.
curio:sand-sarcophagus: a single piece of bone or what seems to be cartilage is inside the old sarcophagus. they say ishtar was buried in a million tombs, each having a part of her.
curio:hieroglyph-slab: ish's edicts. mostly prohibitions, mostly unreadable.
curio:broken-urn: tomb-robber's leftover. whatever was worth taking is long gone.
curio:keystone: the only thing holding the chamber up. remove it and the pyramid finishes burying its dead.
curio:mummy-wrap: the strips shift when you're not watching. the wrapped is not fully dead.
curio:scarab-husk: scarabs re-seal disturbed tombs. this one died mid-repair.
curio:bog-bone: a bone pickled in tannins, swamp-wine.
curio:sunken-log: an empty log, dug out by critters. inside the cavity, a million tiny eggs with bright white dots inside them. reminds you of caviar.
curio:swamp-totem: a scarecrow. lampreylings give it a wide berth; the gar pretend not to see it.
curio:mossy-stone: warm to touch. the bog is cold; this stone isn't, and no one can say why.
curio:witch-bundle: tied herbs. they hum at night. a curse, left for someone she didn't like.
curio:toppled-candle: fire here should not be physically possible. is this a joke from a god?
curio:stained-glass: mural of many fish at a table, with one in the center. the pieces of bread they share form a beautiful melody if sung.
curio:reliquary: a goblet, a silver ring. an antiquarian could offer you many silvers for these trinkets.
curio:wax-pool: the wax cooled in the shape of a face. you almost recognise it.
curio:cracked-pew: the bench is broken. you look at the shards of wood that've broken off but they look... liquid, almost as if the bench melted.
curio:hymnal-scrap: an old hymn to the gods.
curio:polished-stone: you can't make out what this sphere once was. it's perfectly polished, a perfect mirror.
curio:silver-coin: your own. the date and minting match your purse. you don't remember dropping it.
curio:glass-shard: you remember dropping this here. do you?
curio:mirror-frame: shard of the original crystal which formed this very lake you explore.
curio:reflection-pool: you see many versions of yourself in the pool. you see the back of your head, you see your face. you see a stranger.
curio:frozen-fish: patterns frozen into its scales spell a Fis-edict in the old script.
curio:ice-pick: trapper's spare. left for the next walker to find.
curio:frost-flower: a flower of a memory crystal. it remembers sound. touching it, you melt a drop and, as the water drips, you hear a child laughing.
curio:snowdrift-cairn: someone is buried under this snow. nobody marked it on purpose.
curio:walrus-tusk: high-value ivory. the trapper sells these up north.
curio:ship-bell: the drowned captain rang this once, just before the ship went under. the brass still remembers.
curio:porthole: you look through. you see Atlantis, not seabed. the ship sank into their territory.
curio:figurehead: carved to mock the Atlanteans. that's why they sank it.
curio:wave-worn-chest: chest full of booty. you are scared to open it.
curio:rotted-rigging: the crew tied themselves in. the rope shapes match their wrists.
curio:captain-skull: not the captain. the first mate, who took the blame so the captain could keep walking.
curio:star-iron: your village blacksmith would weep to see this. a star-iron.
curio:impact-glass: obsidian variant. cousin to the Inferno's glass; different origin, same cooling.
curio:meteor-fragment: after centuries, still warm. it didn't fully cool when it landed.
curio:cosmic-sigil: the pilgrim cult carved it to call the next meteor down. it's working slowly.
curio:crystal-bloom: memory crystals. kin to the iceshelf frost-flower; these remember sky-sounds before the fall.
curio:cracked-column: this civilisation predates everything you know. the column saw kings.
curio:gladiator-helm: every helm in this dim belongs to someone who lost. the winners are buried with theirs.
curio:marble-bust: nameless. every winner gets one. this hall is endless.
curio:worn-trophy: given to runners-up. you can tell because the engraving is unkind.
curio:banner-pole: a winner cut their own banner down and walked out with it.
curio:mystery-jelly: Khei's first non-water. precedes everything you've ever caught.
curio:lost-lantern: the lantern shines toward you, not away. you cast your shadow into the world.
curio:empty-bottle: the bottle has your handwriting on it. you haven't written this yet.
curio:single-sandal: if you stay here, you leave one shoe behind. it's tradition.
curio:apex-fang: from Fish himself. ineffable god. he gave one as proof he exists.
